hooks.command: PR="${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-.}"; V="$(bash "$PR/scripts/vault-root.sh" 2>/dev/null || echo "$PWD")"; [ -f "$PR/scripts/wiki-lock.sh" ] && WIKI_LOCK_VAULT="$V" bash "$PR/scripts/wiki-lock.sh" clear-stale --max-age 3600 >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

hooks.prompt: If a vault is configured for this session (via the $CLAUDE_OBSIDIAN_VAULT environment variable, a VAULT_PATH in CLAUDE.md, or a wiki/ folder in the current directory), silently read that vault's wiki/hot.md to restore recent context. If wiki/hot.md does not exist, do nothing. This is a non-vault session. Do not announce this. Do not report what you read. Just have the context available.

Hook-injected context does not survive context compaction. If a vault is configured (via the $CLAUDE_OBSIDIAN_VAULT environment variable or a wiki/ folder in the current directory) and its wiki/hot.md exists, silently re-read it now to restore the hot cache. Do not announce this.

hooks.command: PR="${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-.}"; V="$(bash "$PR/scripts/vault-root.sh" 2>/dev/null || echo "$PWD")"; cd "$V" 2>/dev/null || exit 0; { [ -d wiki ] && [ -d .git ]; } || exit 0; CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep '^wiki/' || true); [ -f "$PR/scripts/auto-commit.sh" ] && bash "$PR/scripts/auto-commit.sh" "$V" >/dev/null 2>&1; [ -n "$CHANGED" ] && echo 'WIKI_CHANGED: Wiki pages were modified this session. Please update wiki/hot.md with a brief summary of what changed (under 500 words). Use the hot cache format: Last Updated, Key Recent Facts, Recent Changes, Active Threads. Keep it factual. Overwrite the file completely. It is a cache, not a journal.' || true
